assumptions.role: Supplies a transitive-percolation growth family and physical down-set outcomes.
assumptions.statement: Bernoulli direct-link proposals followed by transitive closure form the order-first microhistory.

assumptions.role: Its Hessian selects the Fisher mobility.
assumptions.statement: Local relative entropy is the path cost on the transition statistical manifold.

assumptions.role: Chooses the sign that favours relation creation and yields the tested marginal natural flow.
assumptions.statement: The source potential is minus expected direct-link count.

assumptions.role: Makes the analytic beta law generate a count hierarchy and is the choice attacked by the path-covariance kill.
assumptions.statement: Log causal cardinality is the candidate flow parameter.

assumptions.role: Keeps the unit map explicit without claiming that either input has been derived here.
assumptions.statement: A microscopic atom length and recovered dimension would map a count hierarchy to a dimensional length.

comparators: antichain versus diamond versus chain causal closure
raw-link Fisher versus physical down-set Fisher
statewise relabeling equivariance versus full path covariance

concept.invariant: A live DU mechanism generates nonuniform effective influence through its own higher-order dynamics rather than fitting a target.

falsifiers: physical transition probabilities or normalized Fisher contributions fail to normalize
event relabeling changes a fixed-state transition probability
N exp(-g) is not invariant under the analytic flow
different natural labelings receive unequal path probability
the claimed hierarchy requires inserting N_* into the update

free_choices.choice: Use one Bernoulli probability for every potential direct link.
free_choices.sensitivity_test: Compare antichain, diamond, and chain causal closures and retain the data-processing loss.
free_choices.why_not_forced: More general classical sequential growth laws have multiple couplings and precursor dependence.

free_choices.choice: Use S=-E[m] and unit natural-flow normalization.
free_choices.sensitivity_test: Expose the choices and check coordinate covariance under h=sinh(g); do not interpret the hierarchy value as predicted.
free_choices.why_not_forced: The sign, local source functional, and time normalization are not selected by the causal order alone.

free_choices.choice: Use illustrative initial couplings generating N*=4,16,64.
free_choices.sensitivity_test: Require N exp(-g) to stay invariant for all three values and report their different length ratios.
free_choices.why_not_forced: No physical law in this probe selects the initial coupling.

free_choices.choice: Use the smallest three-event causet with inequivalent natural birth paths as the covariance kill.
free_choices.sensitivity_test: Require constant-p transitive percolation to pass the identical fixture while count-running p fails.
free_choices.why_not_forced: Many larger causets can test the same path-independence burden.

null_models: an antichain closure preserves every raw direct-link history
constant-p transitive percolation assigns equal path weight on the three-event fixture
a coordinate change h=sinh(g) changes components but not the natural-gradient path
observables: normalized expected Fisher contribution by physical outcome
natural-gradient beta in g and h=sinh(g)
N exp(-g) along the count flow
candidate_contract.question: Can a minimal order-first causal growth law select Fisher mobility, transmute a dimensionless coupling into a count scale, and generate a downstream influence object without violating discrete general covariance?

checks.name: physical down-set transition probabilities normalize
expected down-set Fisher contributions are nonnegative and normalize
fixed-state transition law is exactly relabeling equivariant
causal closure cannot increase Fisher information
antichain closure retains the complete raw-link Fisher information
nontrivial causal closure loses some raw-link Fisher information
KL/Fisher natural descent of the inserted source potential gives beta_g=1
the natural-gradient flow is covariant under h=sinh(g)
N exp(-g) is invariant along the analytic count flow
constant-p transitive percolation passes the three-event path-independence null
cardinality-running p fails the same path-independence null
no Lambda, H0, cosmological age, target geometry, or target concentration enters the probe

result.claim: The fixed-state transition family constructs Fisher mobility, an invariant count-scale skeleton, and a genuine expected-Fisher influence profile, but direct cardinality running makes completed-causet path probabilities depend on natural labeling.
result.remaining_uncertainty: A covariant fixed-coupling renormalization or physical coupling-record extension may evade the kill; geometry recovery, a stable gap, units, response/noise completion, and cosmology remain open.

stop_conditions: Stop the exact feedback law if one natural-labeling counterexample exists.
Do not call the transmuted count a physical length without ell_atom and recovered dimension.
Do not treat expected transition Fisher contribution as Track B observed residual energy.
Do not infer a Lambda identity, value, banked claim, or prediction from the construction.
Local failure remains formalization-scoped rather than a concept kill.
candidate_contract.track: Science Council wild-frontier next actions / HC-DU-011 + HC-DU-022

comparison_receipt.note: Contract completeness means legible and comparable, not physically true. Warrant types are non-ordinal; computation or formal argument disposes.

results.cheap_kill: Enumerate natural labelings of the three-event causet a<c with b isolated. Any candidate feedback must assign them equal total path probability. The count-running fixture fails.

construction.downstream_influence: normalized expected Fisher contributions of physical down-set outcomes
construction.micro_law: Independent Bernoulli direct-link proposals followed by causal transitive closure.
construction.mobility: Fisher metric from local KL on the transition family
construction.physical_outcomes: down-sets used as the past of a new maximal event

results.contested_finding: The HC-DU-011 + HC-DU-022 bridge is wholly unbuilt: no coherent order-first law can yet select a mobility, generate a count scale, and make influence a downstream response object.

count_scale.interpretation: A count hierarchy can be transmuted from a dimensionless initial coupling without inserting N_* into the update. Its value remains an initial-condition output, and a dimensional length still imports ell_atom and requires recovered d.

discrete_general_covariance_kill.final_causet: three events with a<c and b isolated
discrete_general_covariance_kill.reading: Statewise relabeling invariance is weaker than path independence. The cardinality-running coupling makes birth labels observable.

inserted_choices: KL as the local path cost
the sign S=-E[m], favouring relation creation
log cardinality as the flow parameter
unit coefficient in that flow parameter
fisher_natural_flow.mobility_principle: The local KL Hessian supplies Fisher mobility on the Bernoulli transition family.

inserted_choices: KL as the local path cost
the sign S=-E[m], favouring relation creation
log cardinality as the flow parameter
unit coefficient in that flow parameter
the dimension used in the units map
treating raw direct-link proposals as physical microhistory

outcome_fisher.reading: Causal closure is a data-processing map: order structure changes both total observable Fisher response and its influence allocation without fitting a target profile.

relabeling.reading: Each fixed-state transition law and its Fisher contributions are exactly equivariant under event relabeling.

earned: an algebraically coherent statewise order/Fisher/scale skeleton
a true expected-Fisher transition influence decomposition
an exact obstruction to naive birth-count running
not_earned: full classical-sequential-growth covariance for the feedback law
a selected initial coupling or hierarchy
a cosmological observable, Lambda value, banked claim, or seed

results.verdict: CONSTRUCTIVE-SKELETON-FOUND / NAIVE-COUNT-RUNNING-KILLED. KL/Fisher supplies a coordinate-covariant mobility, a dimensionless initial coupling transmutates into an invariant count scale, and the transition law supplies a genuine expected-Fisher influence profile. But letting the coupling run with birth cardinality makes path probabilities depend on natural labeling, so this exact feedback law is not an admissible covariant causal growth law. No physical memory scale, geometry recovery, Lambda identity, claim, or prediction is earned.
